question: [File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadYaml()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a YAML file that sets hiero.mirror.rest.response.limit.max to 0 or a negative value, bypassing the parseDbPoolConfig() validation (which only validates pool keys), causing getResponseLimit() to return a max of 0 and making all paginated API responses return empty results, effectively shutting down >=30% of nodes for data retrieval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadYaml()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a YAML file that sets hiero.mirror.rest.response.limit.default to an extremely large value (e.g., 1000000), causing all unauthenticated requests to default to fetching 1 million rows per page, exhausting DB memory and I/O across >=30% of nodes?
